en: Mmm, so good. I want a cheeseburger.
This is a special soup called pho.
You told me not to say that word. It is delicious.
ko: 그 단어를 말하지 말라고 했잖아요. 정말 맛있어.

en: This is the food of your people. Can I get you anything else?
ko: 이건 당신 나라의 음식이에요. 다른 거 필요하신 거 있으세요?

en: Actually, we're just trying to teach our daughter
about your country.
And as I'm saying that, I'm hoping you're from Vietnam. I was born there. So was Lily.
ko: 이 말을 하면서 베트남 출신이시길 바라고 있어요. 저는 거기서 태어났어요. 릴리도요.

en: Oh, it's a beautiful country. My family still lives there.
ko: 아, 정말 아름다운 나라예요. 가족이 아직 거기 살고 있어요.

en: I hate Vietnam.
Lily, honey, we don't hate. I hate Vietnam.
ko: 릴리야, 우리는 싫어한다고 말하지 않아. 나는 베트남 싫어!

en: Hey, we need just a second.
Lily, that was rude.
I want to go home.
No, it's important that you celebrate
the culture of your ancestors.
You are Vietnamese.
No, I'm not. I'm gay. I'm gay!
Honey, no, you're not gay. You are just confused.
ko: 아니야, 넌 게이가 아니야. 그냥 혼란스러운 거야.

en: Oh, my God, what is wrong with me? It's like I...
ko: 세상에, 나 뭐가 문제야? 나 마치...

en: Oh, please, we have tons of lesbian friends.
ko: 아, 우리 레즈비언 친구 엄청 많잖아.

en: Well, how that you would reference our friends and not us.
ko: 세상에, 친구 얘기를 하지 우리 얘기를 안 하다니.

en: It's okay. We should just go.
No, nobody's going anywhere until she finishes the whole soup. No, Gloria, it's fine.
ko: 아니, 이 국을 다 먹기 전에는 아무도 안 가. 글로리아, 괜찮아.

en: No, no, no, it's not fine.
That's the problem with this country.
Nobody cares where they come from.
ko: 아무도 자기가 어디서 왔는지 신경을 안 써.

en: They just want cheeseburgers and prep school,
and they have forgotten about Colombia.
Okay, I can't help but feel that maybe you're bringing
something to the table that's possibly not at all about Lily.
I think you're right.
It's just that I feel like I'm losing my children to America.
They just going to grow up and become boring old white people Most of our friends are white actually Okay can everybody just calm down and go back to their own conversations Just thank you
Hey. Hey, what's going on with you?
Manny has forgotten most of his Spanish,
and Joe, I'm sure he's not even going to learn it.
I know this is silly, but I just don't like feeling like I'm so different from the rest of my family.
Oh, honey. It feels so lonely.
Wait just a second. Lily, is that why you want to be gay? To be more like your daddies?
Oh, honey, no. The three of us are a family, even though we come from different places.
You know, you were born in Vietnam, and I grew up in a city,
and even though he never talks about it, your daddy grew up on a farm.
The point is, is that we're a family because we love each other. Okay.
And you know what? You just cost a scene in the restaurant,
and you can't get any more like your daddies than that.
Maybe I'll just take Manny and Joe to Colombia this summer.
I think that's a great idea.
I really think that keeping in touch with your heritage
only enriches our culture. Absolutely.
I think we would all be better off
if people would go back to where they came from.
I... I'll pull the car around. Yes.
you
